meta.title: Getting Started
meta.excerpt: New to the documentation? Start here to understand the structure and find the learning path most relevant to your role.

seo.metaDescription: Complete guide to navigating documentation based on your role - CMS Reference for data layer, App Reference for frontend, Infrastructure for DevOps.

blocks.content: For the best understanding of the system, we recommend following this sequential learning path. Each section builds on knowledge from the previous one.

blocks.title: 3-Step Learning Journey
blocks.content:
**Step 1: CMS Reference** → Start with the data layer to understand content modeling and Strapi structure.

**Step 2: App Reference** → Learn how the Next.js frontend consumes and renders CMS data.

**Step 3: Infrastructure & Ops** → Master deployment, monitoring, and operational workflows.

blocks.title: Why This Order?
blocks.content: Understanding the data layer first helps you grasp why the frontend is architected the way it is. Infrastructure knowledge applies after you understand what you're deploying.

features.title: CMS Reference - Start with the Data Layer
features.description: Learn how Strapi content types, dynamic zones, and GraphQL APIs power the entire system. Perfect for backend developers and data architects.

features.title: App Reference - Frontend Fundamentals
features.description: Master the Next.js 15 application with server components, atomic design patterns, and component hydration. Essential for frontend developers.

features.title: Infrastructure & Ops - Deployment & Monitoring
features.description: Understand CI/CD pipelines, PostgreSQL operations, environment configuration, and production troubleshooting. Critical for DevOps engineers.

blocks.title: Why Strapi?

blocks.description: Set up Strapi and create your first content type
